steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A linear function with slope $m$ and y-intercept $b$ is $f(x)=mx+b$. Here $m=69$, and $f(0)=3$ gives $b=3$.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• $f(x)=3x$: slope $3$, not $69$ — wrong slope and wrong intercept.
  • $f(x)=3x+3$: slope $3$, not $69$ — wrong slope.
  • $f(x)=69x$: gives $f(0)=0$, not $3$ — wrong intercept.
  • $f(x)=69x+3$: slope $69$ and $f(0)=3$ — matches.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
